title: tulipa scabriscapa var. strangulata
intro: Tulipa scabriscapa var. strangulata, a type of wild tulip, is a bulbous perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly as a remedy for respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The tulipa scabriscapa var. strangulata contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which have been found to offer health benefits such as relieving congestion and soothing sore throats. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory infections and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and asthma.
